prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene using GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) in gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2) states were computed to derive Fukui indices. The iron center is identified as the most reactive site: f⁺ = 0.22885456 (electrophilic), f⁻ = 0.25938191 (nucleophilic), and dual descriptor = −0.030527. Cyclopentadienyl carbons show much lower reactivity (f⁺ ≈ 0.01364, f⁻ ≈ 0.01218), with hydrogens intermediate. Both cation and anion converged successfully; charge conservation checks (sum f⁺ = 1.0000, sum f⁻ = 1.0000) confirm reliability. Integrity status: trustworthy. Software: xtb; geometry from input xyz file (21 atoms: 10 C, 5 H per ring, 1 Fe). No solvation model applied (gas phase).</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.22885456
